notes: Loose, researched guide to what's trending on short-form right now. The AI uses a phrase ONLY when it genuinely fits the clip, never forced. Refresh by asking Claude Code to 'refresh ClipForge trends', or drop your own list at <clipforge-home>/trends.json (same shape).
general: absolute cinema - for a genuinely insane or beautiful play/clutch
it's giving ___ - name the vibe, e.g. 'it's giving rage quit'
caught in 4k - when you or chat get exposed doing something
lost/gained aura - coolness points after a clutch or a fail
say wallahi - mock-disbelief that this actually happened
in my ___ era - e.g. 'in my villain era' after griefing chat
POV: ___ - frame the clip from your or chat's point of view
tell me why ___ - incredulous setup for an absurd moment
the lion doesn't concern himself with ___ - flexing not caring
nah this is crazy / not me ___ - dry, self-aware framing
tiktok: open with a punchy first-person reaction; dry humor beats polish
end on a comment-bait question (e.g. 'would you have hit this?')
relatable framing ('every <game> player knows this pain')
keep it short and high-energy; one trend phrase max per caption
youtube: front-load the game name + the specific outcome for search
narrative hooks: 'this should not have worked', 'I can't believe this happened', 'watch this impossible ___'
name the exact searchable moment (e.g. 'Tarkov 1v3 extract camp')
ranking / tier-list framing for multi-clip or list videos
